_comment: Golden fixtures for the source-site scrape stderr contract. Shared by the CLI (which must reproduce these line shapes), and by mcp/ + bot/ (whose multi-file parsers must parse them). See cli/CONTRACT.md and the DESIGN contract. Parser rule: every line starting with 'wrote ' yields path = text between 'wrote ' and the LAST ' (' (or rest of line if none); dims = leading WxH of the parenthetical tail if it matches ^\d+x\d+, else null. 'scraped {n} file(s) from {host} into {dir}' is an optional summary. 'error: ...' is an error.

cases.name: two_images
cases._note: Leads with the informational 'scraping …' start line (§3.0); parsers must ignore it and yield exactly the two files below.
cases.stderr:
scraping https://example.com/…
wrote out/logo.png (200x80 px · source example.com)
wrote out/hero.jpg (1280x720 px · source example.com)
scraped 2 file(s) from example.com into out

cases.name: image_and_video
cases.stderr:
wrote assets/pic.png (640x480 px · source cdn.test)
wrote assets/clip.mp4 (source cdn.test)
scraped 2 file(s) from cdn.test into assets

cases.name: path_with_paren
cases.stderr:
wrote out/img (1) (300x300 px · source example.org)
scraped 1 file(s) from example.org into out

cases.name: none_matched
cases.stderr: error: no media matched at https://example.com/
